summary: Iter-134 retest — TESTID FIXES VERIFIED ON LIVE DOM. Both fixes applied to /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/new-procedure.tsx are confirmed working in the rendered DOM at https://dental-workflow-18.preview.emergentagent.com (viewport 400x900, student Gaurav.pandey/Student@123). All five priority testid contracts now present and queryable; all three iter-134 behavioural changes (reorder, overdenture full-arch override, auto-clear) continue to work. Detailed verification: (1) `dropdown-type-of-implant-procedure` ✓ present; option testids `dropdown-type-of-implant-procedure-option-multiple-conventional-implants` etc. all present. (2) FDI testids `fdi-16/17/18/26/27` ALL queryable AND clickable to toggle Missing pills. (3) After marking 16/17 (adjacent cluster) + 26 (singleton), the cluster cards rendered EXACTLY 6 dropdowns total (3 per cluster × 2 clusters): `ridge-contour-17, soft-tissue-17, keratinized-17` for cluster leader=17 and `ridge-contour-26, soft-tissue-26, keratinized-26` for singleton 26 — NOT three sets (no per-tooth dropdowns within a cluster). (4) Tapping `ridge-contour-17` opens dropdown, option testids `ridge-contour-17-option-well-contoured` etc. are queryable, clicking updates trigger label to 'Well Contoured' visibly. (5) Selecting Prosthetic Plan = 'Overdenture with Attachment' via `dropdown-prosthetic-plan-option-overdenture-with-attachment` correctly: hides FDI (fdi-16 disappears), removes per-cluster dropdowns (ridge-contour-* empty), AND renders the custom `overdenture-arch-dropdown` testid in DOM (this confirms the Dropdown component now correctly forwards a passed-in data-testid OVER the auto-generated label-based one). (6) Toggling back to 'Cement Retained Bridge - Metal' restores FDI chart with NO marked teeth (auto-clear preserved).

issues: Console error 'Unexpected text node: . A text node cannot be a child of a <View>.' repeats ~15× across pages — pre-existing (also flagged in iter-126), not introduced by iter-134, no functional impact.

critical_code_review_comments: GOOD: Dropdown signature (new-procedure.tsx L79-88) now accepts both `testID` and a raw `data-testid` (via spread `[key: string]: any`) and uses `customTestId || "dropdown-${labelKebab}"` — exactly the right precedence. Option items (L113-115) emit `${triggerTestId}-option-${optionKebab}` with both testID and data-testid, so options inherit the custom prefix automatically.

GOOD: FDI tooth TouchableOpacity (L1078-1093) now sets BOTH `testID={`fdi-${t}`}` AND `data-testid={`fdi-${t}`}`. RN-Web emits the data-testid attribute as expected (verified: fdi-16/17/18/26/27 all queryable).

MINOR DOC NIT: The original review_request mentioned testids `dropdown-prosthetic-treatment-plan` and an option `option-type-b-knife-edge-ridge` — but the actual field label is 'Prosthetic Plan' (section title is 'Prosthetic Treatment Plan'), so the auto-generated trigger testid is `dropdown-prosthetic-plan`, and the Ridge Contour options are 'Well Contoured / Medium Contoured / Low Contoured' (no 'Type B Knife Edge Ridge'). Tests should reference these actual values; not a code bug.

MINOR: There is no separate `arch-dropdown` testid emitted in the overdenture flow — only `overdenture-arch-dropdown` (which is what the spec asked for). If both names were intended, only the override is wired; otherwise this is fine.

PRE-EXISTING (low priority): The `Unexpected text node: . A text node cannot be a child of a <View>.` warning fires repeatedly. Likely a stray `{cond && ' '}` or trailing space in conditional JSX inside a <View>. Worth a future cleanup pass — does not break functionality.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on testid contracts: 5 of 5 priority testid scenarios verified end-to-end + 3 of 3 iter-134 behavioural regressions still pass. Back-compat save end-to-end (Step 1 → Step 2 → GET /api/procedures/{id} clinical_exam_per_site population) was NOT exercised — per review_request this can be deferred to main-agent self-test post-creation, and the testid contract verification was the gating concern.

test_credentials: Student: Gaurav.pandey / Student@123 (verified login + new-procedure access).
seed_data_creation: None. No POST /api/procedures was finalised — the per-cluster + overdenture testid verification did not require submission.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-134 testid bugs from iter-126 are FIXED. All testids work as designed. For the remaining back-compat save E2E test (clinical_exam_per_site map + legacy ridge_contour fields after POST /api/procedures), the main agent or next testing agent can: (a) login Gaurav.pandey, (b) fill Step 1 with Multiple Conventional Implants + click fdi-16/17/18, (c) set ridge-contour-<leader>='Well Contoured' (or whatever first option), soft-tissue-<leader>='Thin (<=1mm)' or first option, keratinized-<leader>='Adequate (≥2mm)' or first option, (d) skip CBCT if optional or upload mocks, (e) click continue, (f) GET /api/procedures/{id} and assert both `clinical_exam_per_site[<leader>]` and legacy `ridge_contour` fields present. Note actual ridge contour options are 'Well Contoured / Medium Contoured / Low Contoured' (NOT 'Type B Knife Edge Ridge' as the prompt suggested).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — both fixes verified working in DOM. Repro baseline (compare to iter-126): `document.querySelector('[data-testid="overdenture-arch-dropdown"]')` now returns a node (was null in iter-126). `document.querySelector('[data-testid="fdi-16"]')` now returns the TouchableOpacity DOM node (was null in iter-126). `document.querySelectorAll('[data-testid^="ridge-contour-"]').length === 2` after marking adjacent cluster + singleton (was 0 in iter-126 due to label collision). All match expectations from action_items[1] + action_items[2] of iter-126.
